error_signature: Header name must be a valid HTTP token [“Header Auth account”]
title: Invalid HTTP token in header authentication
symptom: When executing a workflow with the PerplexityAI API, an error occurs stating 'Header name must be a valid HTTP token [“Header Auth account”]'. This prevents the workflow from running successfully.
not_this_if: Header name is set correctly to 'Authorization'
root_cause: The error occurs because the header name used for authentication is incorrect. Instead of using a valid HTTP token name like 'Authorization', an invalid name such as 'Header Auth account' was used, leading to the error.
fix_steps: Open the credentials settings for the PerplexityAI API node.
Change the header name from 'Header Auth account' to 'Authorization'.
Ensure the value is set to 'Bearer {API_KEY}' with the actual API key.
Save the changes and try executing the workflow again.
agent_instruction: Update the header name to 'Authorization' and set the value to 'Bearer {API_KEY}'.
